abstract: As anthropogenic climate change poses an escalating threat to global ecological stability, there is an urgent need to scrutinize and mitigate the environmental footprint of the healthcare sector. Medical imaging, in particular, represents a substantial source of clinical carbon emissions. This article examines the critical environmental challenges facing the field and illustrates practical, sustainable interventions. By providing actionable examples of green practices, we aim to empower radiologists to lead decarbonization efforts and implement meaningful systemic changes within their departments.
